untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options strategy (calendar spread with short-term and longer-term calls) that requires tracking derivative positions, expiration dates, and option-specific mechanics (expires worthless, time decay). The allowed indicators are designed for equity price/technical analysis only and cannot express: (1) option position establishment and management, (2) option expiration status, (3) time-decay dynamics between two different expiration dates, or (4) the relationship between underlying price movement and multi-leg derivative P&L. A proper translation would require derivative-specific indicators and position-tracking fields not available in this equity-focused indicator set.
translation_notes: The signal is fundamentally about options strategy timing rather than price action. While we could approximate 'stock below strike at initiation' as close < pivot and 'rally above strike' as close > pivot, this loses the critical context: the profitability mechanics depend on the short call expiring worthless (time decay) and the longer call retaining value (time value preservation), which cannot be expressed with price-based indicators alone.
